title: Wedelia pseudoyedaea
intro: Wedelia pseudoyedaea is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family, Asteraceae, commonly known as a type of wild or Mexican creeping marigold. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Wedelia pseudoyedaea include flavonoids, terpenoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Wedelia pseudoyedaea is typically used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Wedelia pseudoyedaea is a member of the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Wedelia which is a group of flowering plants. This genus is further classified under the tribe Heliantheae, which is a subgroup of the Asteraceae family. The Wedelia pseudoyedaea is native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of Wedelia pseudoyedaea is also sometimes referred to as Eclipta pseudoyedaea in various sources.
